subject: ap-psychology
title: Research Methods & Experimental Design
stimulus:
Malia has an assignment to write a research paper for a class. She has several weeks to work on the paper
before turning in the finished product.

parts.prompt: Explain how Albert Bandura’s theory of self-efficacy could apply to the scenario.

scoring_points.description:
The response must indicate that Malia believes that she has the ability to write a good  paper and that facilitates her finishing the assignment. 

OR 

The response can indicate that a lack of belief in her ability to write a good paper makes  it less likely she would finish the assignment.

required_elements: Malia believes she can write a good paper, so she does her research and gets a good grade from her teacher.
alternatives.correct_example: Malia believes she can write a good paper, so she does her research and gets a good grade from her teacher.

required_elements: Malia is unsure whether she can write a good paper, so she procrastinates and ends
alternatives.correct_example: Malia is unsure whether she can write a good paper, so she procrastinates and ends

wrong_examples: Malia is confident about her paper.

parts.prompt: Explain how A low score on the Big Five trait of neuroticism could apply to the scenario.

scoring_points.description: The response must indicate a description of low neuroticism and connect it to  completion of the paper (either helping her or hindering her).

required_elements: Malia exhibits a sense of calm so she can complete her project on time.
alternatives.correct_example: Malia exhibits a sense of calm so she can complete her project on time.

wrong_examples: Malia is very diligent, which helps her complete the project.

parts.prompt: Explain how External locus of control could apply to the scenario.

scoring_points.description: The response must indicate that Malia believes that outcomes are determined by forces  outside of her control (e.g., fate, luck, destiny, circumstances, other people) and that  affects her completion of the paper.

required_elements: Malia thinks that fate determines her life, so she doesn’t take proactive steps to do her paper.
alternatives.correct_example: Malia thinks that fate determines her life, so she doesn’t take proactive steps to do her paper.

required_elements: Malia thinks that random factors determine her life, so she doesn’t put in the effort to complete the assignment.
alternatives.correct_example: Malia thinks that random factors determine her life, so she doesn’t put in the effort to complete the assignment.

required_elements: Malia believes that the teacher is biased against her and so she doesn’t even try to complete the paper.
alternatives.correct_example: Malia believes that the teacher is biased against her and so she doesn’t even try to complete the paper.

wrong_examples: Malia knows that she controls her destiny.
Malia is worried that she will have bad luck.
Malia has no control over how her paper is graded, so she could do poorly.

parts.prompt: Explain how Divergent thinking could apply to the scenario.

scoring_points.description: The response must indicate that Malia generates multiple or creative solutions, and this  affects her completion of the paper.

required_elements: Malia considers many different topics for her research paper/uses an innovative approach to help her to do well on the assignment.
alternatives.correct_example: Malia considers many different topics for her research paper/uses an innovative approach to help her to do well on the assignment.

wrong_examples: Malia knows there is one best way to write a good paper.

parts.prompt: Explain how Efferent neurons could apply to the scenario.

scoring_points.description: The response must indicate that efferent (motor) neurons contribute to some element of  motor movement which affects Malia’s completion of her paper.

required_elements: Malia’s efferent neurons help her move her fingers, so she can type her paper.
alternatives.correct_example: Malia’s efferent neurons help her move her fingers, so she can type her paper.

wrong_examples: Malia’s efferent neurons help her feel where the paper is.

parts.prompt: Malia’s teacher, Mr. Barry, has many papers to grade. Explain how each of the following might apply to his success in grading the papers. Explain how Incentive theory relates to the scenario.

scoring_points.description: The response must indicate that a desired activity or an external reward motivates Mr.  Barry, and this helps him complete the grading.

required_elements: Mr. Barry sets a goal that if he completes his grading, he can watch his favorite television show.
alternatives.correct_example: Mr. Barry sets a goal that if he completes his grading, he can watch his favorite television show.

required_elements: After every five papers he corrects, Mr. Barry rewards himself with five minutes on his favorite social media account.
alternatives.correct_example: After every five papers he corrects, Mr. Barry rewards himself with five minutes on his favorite social media account.

wrong_examples: Mr. Barry really wants to give his students feedback, so he grades all the papers in

parts.prompt: Malia’s teacher, Mr. Barry, has many papers to grade. Explain how each of the following might apply to his success in grading the papers. Explain how Crystallized intelligence relates to the scenario.

scoring_points.description: The response must indicate that accumulated knowledge (facts, general information, or  vocabulary) helps Mr. Barry successfully grade the papers or that a lack of accumulated  knowledge hinders his success.

required_elements: Mr. Barry can grade papers quickly because he is able to remember lots of facts about the topic he is grading.
alternatives.correct_example: Mr. Barry can grade papers quickly because he is able to remember lots of facts about the topic he is grading.

required_elements: Mr. Barry is wise because he has learned so much in his life, which helps him grade papers faster.
alternatives.correct_example: Mr. Barry is wise because he has learned so much in his life, which helps him grade papers faster.

wrong_examples: Mr. Barry grades papers faster because he is an experienced grader.
Mr. Barry is capable of thinking very quickly and thus can grade a large number of
